conditioning: ANALYTIC: tense/aspect is a free particle before the verb; Indonesian has no inflectional tense

fit_none.note: Indonesian has NO verb tense morphology; these free aspect particles are the system (the meN-/di- prefixes are VOICE, not tense)

evidence_citations.claim: aspect is marked by free particles

prose: ind (analytic) marks tense/aspect with FREE PARTICLES, not affixes: sudah/telah (perfective/'already'), akan (future), sedang (progressive). Recovered from each particle's English alignment (akan→will, sudah→have, sedang→was). Note: the meN-/di- prefixes are VOICE, not tense — Indonesian has no inflectional tense. Recommendation: PROMOTE the analytic aspect particles.
